Now, code:

class Solution {
public:
    bool checkSubarraySum(vector<int>& nums, int k) {
        unordered_map<int, int> remainder_map;
        remainder_map[0] = -1; // initial state
        int current_sum = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < nums.size(); ++i) {
            current_sum += nums[i];
            int remainder = current_sum % k;
            // Check if remainder exists in the map
            if (remainder_map.find(remainder) != remainder_map.end()) {
                // if the current index - stored index >= 2
                if (i - remainder_map[remainder] >= 2) {
                    return true;
                }
            } else {
                remainder_map[remainder] = i;
            }
        }
        return false;
    }
};

Implementing in C++:

But in C++, the modulus operator % returns a non-negative result only if the dividend is positive, which it is here. So no problem.

Now, code:

class Solution {
public:
    bool checkSubarraySum(vector<int>& nums, int k) {
        unordered_map<int, int> remainder_map;
        remainder_map[0] = -1; // initial state
        int current_sum = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < nums.size(); ++i) {
            current_sum += nums[i];
            int remainder = current_sum % k;
            // Check if remainder exists in the map
            if (remainder_map.find(remainder) != remainder_map.end()) {
                // if the current index - stored index >= 2
                if (i - remainder_map[remainder] >= 2) {
                    return true;
                }
            } else {
                remainder_map[remainder] = i;
            }
        }
        return false;
    }
};
